This move aims to support those facing financial stress due to weather-related disruptions.

The bank's special mention accounts in the MSME portfolio having exposure of ₹1 crore and above reduced to 1.05% of the MSME loan book in March, at ₹487 crore, as against ₹582 crore in December 2025, he said.

items.title: India invokes WTO peace clause for FY25 rice subsidies for farmers
items.summary: India has once again utilized the World Trade Organisation peace clause. This is the seventh time the country has invoked this provision. It allows India to exceed the 10% subsidy limit for rice farmers. The government informed the WTO about providing subsidies worth $7.6 billion. This represents about 11.85% of the total value of rice production.

items.title: Brazil Probe Ties JBS, Cargill to Vendors Linked to Slave Labor
items.summary: JBS NV and Cargill Agrícola SA are defendants in a public civil action brought by Brazil prosecutors after authorities determined the companies had systematically sourced supplies from vendors linked to slavery-like labor conditions.

items.title: Exclusive: RBI Gold Monetisation Scheme—Earn from idle gold at home, rules explained
items.summary: Exclusive: Gold monetisation scheme revamp may allow households to earn from idle gold at home, with new proposals to make the scheme simpler and more attractive. With over 20,000 tonnes of gold lying unused, policymakers and industry bodies are pushing reforms to reduce imports and turn gold into a productive asset.

items.title: Uncertainty prevails over El Nino’s potential strengths, says Australian Met body

Companies like Mondelez, Coca-Cola, Pernod Ricard, Reckitt Benckiser, and Carlsberg reported double-digit growth in the March quarter. This follows a period of volatility. Recent GST changes are now helping consumption. India remains a key growth market for these international brands.

For every $1 per barrel increase in crude prices, fuel marketing losses rise by about 60 paise per litre, assuming no change in retail prices.

items.summary: India is preparing for a third Jan Vishwas Bill. This initiative aims to simplify business processes and reduce compliance burdens. items.summary: Public sector banks and financial institutions are now required to offer reservations for individuals with benchmark disabilities in direct recruitment and promotions. Despite the recent dip, credit growth has maintained double digits for over seven months. This indicates a sustained revival in demand from both individuals and businesses.

The vehicle failed to meet advertised performance benchmarks. The Uttarakhand Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission ruled this an unfair trade practice. The buyer was deceived by inaccurate specifications. The firm must return the vehicle.

Extraordinarily high fuel costs are increasingly reflected in ticket prices.  This situation is testing airline resilience.  Regulators are urged to grant flexibility on slots due to extraordinary circumstances.  March traffic and forward bookings remain unaffected so far.

The economy is projected to recover in the latter half of the year. Nomura forecasts a full-year growth of 6.8%. Supportive policies and easing trade tensions are positive factors. The Iran conflict poses a potential risk.
